description: Operational weights for turning broadcast retrospective labels into next-day editorial guidance.

label_actions.used_as_lead: Boost stories that explain the open, are understandable in five minutes, and have slide-ready evidence.
label_actions.used_as_slide: Prefer candidates with concrete PPT assets over text-only summaries when editorial strength is similar.
label_actions.used_as_talk_only: Keep related material concise and spoken-only unless the visual has independent explanatory value.
label_actions.strong_broadcast_fit: Repeat the evidence mix that matched the actual broadcast: fact/data plus a usable visual.
label_actions.weak_broadcast_fit: Use as a backup pattern, not as a lead pattern.
label_actions.not_used_too_complex: Demote stories that require too much setup for the first five minutes.
label_actions.not_used_low_visual_value: Do not force a slide for material that works better as one sentence.
label_actions.not_used_already_known: Check prepricing and whether the audience has already absorbed the story.
label_actions.not_used_weak_market_reaction: Require a visible market reaction before promoting to lead.
label_actions.missed_source_gap: Add source/search watchpoints for topics that appeared in the broadcast but were absent from the dashboard.
label_actions.missed_weighting_error: Reduce weight on similar weak signals and require a stronger fact/data anchor.
label_actions.false_positive_sentiment_only: Never promote X/Reddit/community-only material as fact or as a lead.
label_actions.false_positive_visual_only: Do not infer causality from a chart or heatmap without fact/data/analysis support.

hard_rules: Retrospective feedback is preference and format guidance, not a source for new market facts.
A positive label can boost presentation fit, but current-day evidence still decides the story.
A negative label should create a warning or demotion unless current-day fact/data evidence clearly overrides it.
